title: Smallest Palindromic Rearrangement II
performanceTarget: top 1% execution time and overall performance
note: C++ proposals for this challenge. All variants are in this folder because native integer combinatorics and fixed arrays are the best fit for this k-th multiset permutation workload.

variants.languageReason: C++ provides low-overhead fixed arrays, inlined capped-combinatorics helpers, and cheap string construction; this keeps the greedy multiset-permutation solution fast while using only a compact binomial table.
variants.approachJustification: Build only the left half. Try letters lexicographically and count the remaining half permutations with capped multinomial products from a compact C(n,r) table.
variants.timeComplexity: O(A^2 * m + R * m), effectively O(n) for A=26 and R=32

variants.languageReason: C++ 64-bit arithmetic and fixed alphabet loops make the multinomial block-ratio method branch-light and allocation-minimal, which is ideal for top runtime on n <= 1e4.
variants.approachJustification: Compute one exact-enough current multinomial count per position, then derive candidate block sizes by total * count[c] / remaining instead of recounting each tentative branch.
variants.timeComplexity: O(A * m + R * m), effectively O(n) for A=26 and R=32

variants.languageReason: C++ keeps the minimum-footprint variant in scalar arithmetic and a 26-slot count array; no VM, GC, hash table, or large combinatorics table is needed.
variants.approachJustification: Use the same deterministic block-ratio greedy as the runtime variant, but compute capped binomial values on demand with at most 32 multiplicative steps.
variants.timeComplexity: O(A * R * m + A * m), effectively O(n) for fixed A=26 and R=32
variants.spaceComplexity: O(n), with O(1) auxiliary state beyond returned strings

reasoningSummary: Distinct palindromic rearrangements correspond exactly to distinct permutations of the half-count multiset. A lexicographic greedy kth-permutation construction chooses each left-half character by counting the size of the block it would create. Counts are saturated at k or k * remaining, avoiding overflow and big integers.
timeComplexity: O(n) effective for all variants under fixed alphabet and capped binomial width
spaceComplexity: O(R * m + n) recommended/runtime, O(n) memory
